Arc Flash Study

Find Out What It's All About

CareLabs offers organizations electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services at a reasonable price. Arc flash threats, personnel training, documentation, and PPE requirements will be evaluated to ensure your electrical safety program is compliant. CareLabs can rapidly identify arc flash dangers and take the necessary steps to reduce the impact of harmonic distortions to ensure the equipment’s safety. Arc flashes are analyzed and studied using the software ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program). 

Enquire

What is Involved

An arc flash is the amount of heat and light produced by an arc fault. This occurs when current travels across an air gap between two conductors. The arc flash emits a substantial amount of light and heat, ionizing the surrounding air (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). As part of an arc flash investigation, the electrical systems of a facility are evaluated and analyzed to determine the incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. Arc flash research promotes safe working conditions through the development of safety recommendations for qualified electricians working on equipment and circuits. In addition, there are updated one-line electrical schematics, equipment locations, a short circuit and coordination analysis, and more. 

Arc Flash Research Goals:

  • Determine the degree of the damage caused by the incident energy emitted during an arc fault.
  • Modifying the settings of protective equipment to decrease exposure to incident energy
  • Develop safety standards for arc flash risks
  • Conform with your country’s rules and legislation.
  • Identify the type of PPE utilized on the project.

Negative Effects of an Arc Flash:

  • Arcs can cause severe burns to the skin, face, and eyes.
  • Inhaled gases and particles can cause serious burns to the trachea and lungs.
  • Accidents caused by flying items and loose parts

Arc flash investigations are vital for reducing hazards and enhancing the safety and security of electrical staff and the firm. Arc flash research assists in identifying electrical danger levels and implementing the appropriate safety measures to reduce the risk of burns and accidents. All utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial organizations that employ electrical disconnects, motor control, panel boards, and switches must do arc flash analysis. 

Arc Flash Analysis Advantages

  • Improved human and machine safety
  • Reduced medical and legal costs
  • Compliance with safety-related statutory requirements
  • A reduction in the number of code infractions and fines
  • Following techniques are utilized for arc flash analysis:
  • Data collection
  • Conducting site visits
  • A system’s design
  • Investigating arc flashes

Work Method of Arc Flash Research in CareLabs

CareLabs provides superior services and will fulfill any requests from the construction crew, a neighboring contractor, or an engineering company.

These are the steps in our work procedure:

  • Data collection
  • ETAP modelling for an electric power system
  • Examination of the short-circuit
  • An evaluation of the collaboration of overcurrent protection device
  • Studies on arc flashes
  • Conducting arc flash tests
  • Preserving a record
  • Methods for reducing arc flash hazard
  • Large single-line diagrams
  • Displaying arc flash caution indicators
  • The finalized record

Advantages of Using CareLabs’ Services:

  • Develop electrical systems that are safer, better controlled, and comply with standards.
  • Bus ratings and protection devices are combined with modules for comprehensive short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device evaluation, and arc flash analysis.
  • Enhancing workplace safety
  • Simple and rapid alternative design suggestion
  • Increased safety margins since arc fault limits are user-defined
  • Use our computer-aided analysis to produce arc flash labels automatically to save time.
  • Reduce maintenance expenses and losses in productivity.

Crew members are strategically positioned throughout the CareLabs to ensure our specialists are available in a regular or urgent incident. CareLabs has become an ISO 9001:2008-accredited firm with good client feedback fairly swiftly. In addition to Zurich, Geneva, Basel, and Bern we offer investigation and analysis services for arc flash in all other major cities. Contact us immediately for a free quote on our arc flash analysis and study services.

Harmonic Study and Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

CareLabs offers comprehensive research and analysis on harmonics in addition to electrical installation services including safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification. Professionals will evaluate the harmonic dangers, training materials, paperwork, and personal protective equipment (PPE) of your electrical safety program to ensure that it is current, exhaustive, and compliant. CareLabs can detect harmonics early on and take the necessary steps to decrease the impact of harmonic distortions to ensure the equipment’s safety. 

Enquire

What is Involved

Due to harmonic voltages and currents, an electrical system might become ineffective. They emit inefficient heat and may degrade the quality of the generated power. The mathematical technique of harmonic voltage analysis is used to predict resonances and harmonic distortion levels. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical approach for predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on empirical data. 

Nonlinear electrical switching equipment such as AC variable frequency motors, DC drives, fluorescent lights, and computers introduce harmonic frequencies to an electrical system. High voltage/current harmonic levels increase the risk of equipment failure. Consequently, prompt study and analysis of harmonics are essential. Currently, systems utilizing 415 volts have harmonic limits of 5%, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics. 

Harmonic Distortion Consequences:

  • Responsible for high voltage and circulating current.
  • Device failure and destruction because of severe voltage distortion
  • A rise in the internal energy of components, which leads to component failure and a shortened lifespan.
  • Fault-induced tripping of branch circuit breakers
  • Measurement flaw
  • Electrical and distribution system fires and explosions
  • Generator failures
  • A reduction in system power factor
  • Installation and utility costs are costly
  • Productivity reductions
  • Inactivity and needless driving
  • Use of bigger motors

Harmonic difficulties are extremely uncommon, but when they do occur, the dependability, quality, and efficiency of the power system tend to deteriorate. Therefore, harmonic study and analysis is essential.

  • Protects against voltage and current harmonics
  • Voltage and current imbalances.
  • Restriction of frequency volatility.
  • Improves power factor analysis and keeps results near to one.
  • Controls change in voltage.

With the use of harmonic research and analysis, which will provide light on the origin and impact of power quality concerns, it will be simpler to avoid harmonic-related problems during the design phase and less likely that unintended consequences would emerge. 

Workflow of Carelabs for Harmonic Analysis and Research:

  • Highlight available nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and lengthy medium-voltage cables on the electrical single-line diagram.
  • Determine the PCC (point of common coupling).
  • Locate system buses inside the facility that are sensitive to harmonic distortions.
  • Get harmonics information for all nonlinear loads.
  • Get vital information on voltage and current harmonics at the selected PCC, including the greatest and lowest levels of short circuit faults.
  • Do a harmonic analysis of the electrical network using ETAP.
  • Determine the overall and individual current and voltage wave distortion values at the buses and PCC.
  • Sketch the spectrum of harmonic frequencies.
  • If harmonic distortion levels surpass acceptable thresholds, a suitable mitigation strategy is offered and implemented.
  • After implementing the mitigation solution, rerun the analysis.

Advantages of Carelabs's Offerings:

If background harmonics have been discovered earlier, a higher rating is applied in the second step. 

  • Stage 2 allows variable converter size dependent on fault level and converter type if the background is less than twenty-five percent.
  • If the background is between 25% and 75%, 50% of the authorized converter grade may be utilized.
  • On the top background, a comprehensive harmonic analysis is performed.

CareLabs has personnel stationed in a variety of locations to ensure that our specialists are readily accessible in the event of a routine or emergency crisis. CareLabs has swiftly established itself as an organization with ISO 9001:2008 certification and a dedicated customer base that has provided outstanding feedback. We do harmonic research and analysis in several important Swiss cities, including Zurich, Geneva, Basel, and Bern. Contact us immediately to organize your harmonic analysis and study in Swiss or to receive a quote.

  • Carelabz Switzerland

  • Motor Start Analysis

Motor Start Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

CareLabs provides Swiss-based businesses with specialized Motor start research and analysis services. The primary objective of our motor start analysis service is to evaluate the peculiar consequences of starting a large motor. In addition to correctly connecting an electric motor to the line voltage, there are several additional aspects to examine before starting it, including nuisance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, which are all indications of a faulty power supply.

Enquire

What is Involved

CareLabs will send qualified personnel to your area to collect data on recent and historical motor installations. Then, calculations are performed to predict future consequences, and recommendations for mitigating such effects are provided. These calculations are done with the acquired data and manufacturer input. 

Using both static and dynamic modelling methodologies, motor starting procedures may be evaluated. 

  • The measured starting impedance of the electric motor is modelled to identify the optimal load flow solution.
  • In pre-fault short circuit conditions, the short-circuit technique is utilized for motor starting currents, the voltage drop method is utilized.
  • If an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics are available, the dynamic model of the electric motor may be utilized to estimate the impact of motor starting in a conventional transient stability approach.
  • In the absence of thorough dynamic model information for an electric motor, simulations of transient stability may be utilized.

A Motor Start Analysis is Required in Swiss:

Modern industrial systems are more dependent on motors, and the size of electric motors is growing. When a large motor connected to a line is started, it might cause interference with surrounding loads, buses, and the electric motor. When starting an electric motor, the terminal voltage must be at 80% of the rated voltage or higher. Since the square of a motor’s terminal voltage is exactly proportional to torque, voltage difference influences the load torque of the motor.  

Running machines may approach their breaking torque and slow down or halt in the case of a severe voltage fall. Numerous products, including lighting loads, control systems, and electrical equipment, are affected by voltage drops. In addition, a large initial current reduces terminal voltage, hence increasing the current drawn by all systems supplying a continuous load. Furthermore, problematic low-voltage relays are found. 

When Is a Motor Start Analysis Required?

  • Prior to or during the acquisition of a large motor.
  • If there is no available generator and the motor rating exceeds 30 percent of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
  • If the motor rating exceeds 10 to 15 percent of the generator’s KVA rating and the generator is the only source of power for the system. A motor start study may be required if many motors start simultaneously in an industrial setting.
  • When starting a motor with a large capacity, the capacity constraints of smaller power supply make voltage loss concerns even more difficult. It is simpler to comprehend the output of systems that employ tiny generators if one examines how motors start up.

Motor Start Analysis's advantages

Analysis of a motor’s startup utilizing a motor or starting technique with the optimum speed-torque characteristics is desirable. Any unusual loads must be accelerated under closely monitored conditions to avoid exceeding the equipment’s maximum permissible torque. It is possible to verify that the motor’s shaft is operating at any time by performing a motor start study. As a result, it aids in determining the maximum permitted torque for the apparatus. 

Motor starting research determines if an electric motor can start and accelerate, and the machine’s voltage. The skilled personnel at CareLabs conduct motor start studies to comprehensively evaluate your electric motor. We provide motor start analysis services in every major Swiss city, including Zurich, Geneva, Basel, and Bern. Contact us immediately for price information or to arrange your motor starting study.

Motor Start Analysis
Motor Start Analysis
Find Out What It's All About

CareLabs provides Swiss-based businesses with specialized Motor start research and analysis services. The primary objective of our motor start analysis service is to evaluate the peculiar consequences of starting a large motor. In addition to correctly connecting an electric motor to the line voltage, there are several additional aspects to examine before starting it, including nuisance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, which are all indications of a faulty power supply.

Enquire
What is Involved

CareLabs will send qualified personnel to your area to collect data on recent and historical motor installations. Then, calculations are performed to predict future consequences, and recommendations for mitigating such effects are provided. These calculations are done with the acquired data and manufacturer input. 

Using both static and dynamic modelling methodologies, motor starting procedures may be evaluated. 

The measured starting impedance of the electric motor is modelled to identify the optimal load flow solution.
In pre-fault short circuit conditions, the short-circuit technique is utilized for motor starting currents, the voltage drop method is utilized.
If an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics are available, the dynamic model of the electric motor may be utilized to estimate the impact of motor starting in a conventional transient stability approach.
In the absence of thorough dynamic model information for an electric motor, simulations of transient stability may be utilized.
A Motor Start Analysis is Required in Swiss:

Modern industrial systems are more dependent on motors, and the size of electric motors is growing. When a large motor connected to a line is started, it might cause interference with surrounding loads, buses, and the electric motor. In addition, a large initial current reduces terminal voltage, hence increasing the current drawn by all systems supplying a continuous load. Furthermore, problematic low-voltage relays are found.

Prior to or during the acquisition of a large motor.
If there is no available generator and the motor rating exceeds 30 percent of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
If the motor rating exceeds 10 to 15 percent of the generator’s KVA rating and the generator is the only source of power for the system. A motor start study may be required if many motors start simultaneously in an industrial setting.
When starting a motor with a large capacity, the capacity constraints of smaller power supply make voltage loss concerns even more difficult. It is simpler to comprehend the output of systems that employ tiny generators if one examines how motors start up.
Motor Start Analysis's advantages

Any unusual loads must be accelerated under closely monitored conditions to avoid exceeding the equipment’s maximum permissible torque. It is possible to verify that the motor’s shaft is operating at any time by performing a motor start study. As a result, it aids in determining the maximum permitted torque for the apparatus. 

Motor starting research determines if an electric motor can start and accelerate, and the machine’s voltage. The skilled personnel at CareLabs conduct motor start studies to comprehensively evaluate your electric motor. We provide motor start analysis services in every major Swiss city, including Zurich, Geneva, Basel, and Bern. Contact us immediately for price information or to arrange your motor starting study.

  • Power Quality Analysis

Power Quality Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

CareLabs examines the quality of electricity for Swiss businesses. Our objective is to protect your electrical appliances, electrical installations, and electrical equipment from problems caused by an unreliable power supply.

Enquire

What is Involved

Corporations and a rising number of individuals rely on data collection, monitoring, and measurement equipment that is extremely susceptible to fluctuations in power supply. Little electrical equipment is intended to manage the voltage fluctuation spectrum, which is a common characteristic of distributed energy. 

CareLabs can assist you with power quality issues pertaining to equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning. 

Evaluation of Power Quality

  • Evaluation of load-to-electrical-system mismatches
  • Analyzing the workplace to improve simulation approaches for power quality
  • Predicting the operation and efficacy of load equipment
  • Enhance the reliability and power factor of the device.
  • Limit your energy utilization.
  • To avoid failures and disruptions.
  • Reducing power quality anomalies such as transients, spikes, and harmonics.
  • Contribute to the prevention of PLC lockups and voltage/frequency spikes.
  • To avoid capacitor explosions, transformer overheating, and nuisance tripping, among other things.
  • Significantly decreased utility costs
  • Enhance the strength of the electrical network and its components.
  • Evaluate the resilience to variation of electrical equipment.

Switzerland Requires an Analysis of Power Quality

All electrical networks are susceptible to supply interruptions, which occur more often when storms, accidents, or other natural disasters destroy power wires. Power supply fluctuations are quite common. There are three distinct forms of variations, disturbances, and distortions that can affect the quality of power provided by a network.

1. A frequency deviates from its range when it exceeds or falls below its typical range.

2. When the voltage form is kept but the voltage amplitude is larger than usual, there are voltage disruptions.

2.1 Voltage spikes and dips lasting less than one minute are examples of transitory variations.

2.2 Under voltages and over voltages that persist longer than a minute are both called long duration voltage fluctuations.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage: This problem emerges when the load is spread unevenly between the phases, resulting in significant equipment heating.

2.4 Voltage differences between neutral and ground

3. Disturbances caused by voltage distortion, which occurs when the voltage’s shape is not sinusoidal nor uniform.

3.1 Device heating, increased losses, poor power utilization, and resonance difficulties are all produced by harmonics and inter-harmonics.

3.2 Transients of short duration that can damage switch contact surfaces, reduce system performance, and expedite the failure of motor drives and control.

What is Included in a Power Quality Analysis?

It requires several approaches to notice distinct power quality concerns, including the ones described below:  

  • Harmonic evaluation: This method combines reduction techniques in addition to computer algorithms to locate and anticipate future harmonic difficulties.
  • During this investigation, fluctuations and surges will be observed.
  • This research would examine the voltage’s value and trajectory, as well as its short-term increase and decline.
  • Assessment of Reactive Force Through this analysis, the preferred reactive power at the distribution and load ends will be identified.
  • Analysis of Captive Electricity: This project would monitor and coordinate captive electricity to satisfy demand with fewer fuel use and energy surcharges.
  • Load Flow Analysis: This study anticipates the quantities of power flow, power factor, voltage levels, and losses in system branches using the operating data supplied.

CareLabs provides the highest quality service to improve the quality and dependability of your electrical installation for the benefit of all its clients. In all major cities, including Zurich, Geneva, Basel, and Bern we conduct power quality assessments. Contact us immediately to book a power quality test or to receive a quote.

Power System Study and Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

Electrical safety inspections, electrical system design simulations, power quality analysis studies, and power system study and analysis are just a few of the electrical installation services that CareLabs delivers. Our group of electrical specialists employs ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the most useful tool for power system transient analysis, to carry out load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies. It enables us to work directly with customers to build and execute the finest power compensation and solutions. 

Enquire

The Value of Doing a Power System Analysis

Every industrial and commercial structure must have a sturdy, dependable, and well-maintained electrical system. Even a momentary interruption of electricity can have severe implications, leading to protracted downtime, damage to key apparatus, and costly repairs. A continual evaluation of your facility’s electrical system may reveal that it is reliable and secure under both normal and abnormal conditions.

At every stage of the design, installation, and operation of a power system, detailed investigation is necessary. ETAP will be utilized in our studies and evaluations of power systems to provide an overview of the system’s performance during both normal operation and transient fault scenarios. It represents a collection of original engineering analytical studies used to examine how an electric power system responds to events occurring over varying time periods.

Multiple tests indicate the state of the electrical system at your institution. A study report on power system analysis might include the following examples:

  • Circuit breaker
  • Coordination of safety procedures
  • Transit of Load
  • Dynamic and transient assessment
  • Arc Flash Examination
  • Power Efficiency
  • study of harmonics
  • Starting the engine
  • Earthing studies

It allows enterprises to set a baseline for other parts of overall plant management, including the electrical safety program. These investigations indicate the possibility of safe and reliable operations. 

Examination of Load Flow

Using load flow studies, it is feasible to determine the voltages and currents in various network segments and select the appropriate equipment for the task. ETAP’s built-in load flow function may be used to perform load flows, which provide information on a power system’s power loading, power factor, voltages, and currents. 

Short-Circuit Investigation:

A power system must do a short-circuit analysis to comprehend how faulty circumstances emerge. Manually performing short circuit experiments requires considerable work utilizing software like ETAP is significantly more efficient. ETAP is utilized to simulate system protection devices in order to determine the effects of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures in distribution systems. 

Short-Term Study:

Lightning strikes and switching operations cause transient over voltages and currents, which are identified by transient investigations. The results outline the required levels of insulation and surge protection. 

Investigations into Protection Coordination:

This research attempts to validate the system’s security components. This ensures that they are synchronized and appropriate for the apparatus they are guarding.

The following essential functions are often incorporated into our power system analysis procedure:

  • Data collecting method
  • Analysis of data and client meetings
  • accumulating equipment data
  • Model of a single-line diagram
  • ETAP power system modelling
  • Analysis of short circuit and load flow
  • Investigations into protection cooperation
  • Arc flash evaluation
  • A summary of the study report.
  • Risk assessment and mitigation planning
  • The administration of a project.

The following components make up the CareLabs Power System Analysis:

  • Options for reducing energy prices and consumption.
  • Highlighting issue areas in reports.
  • Ideas for solutions
  • Details on how and where energy is utilized.
  • Verification of safety device ratings
  • Help in preventing damage and injury.
  • The effects of harmonics and transients

As part of our power system analysis studies, we analyze the client’s electrical system and then provide recommendations for improving its efficiency and supply security. Zurich, Geneva, Basel, and Bern are served by our power system analysis and study services. Contact us immediately for a quote or to organize a study and analysis of your power system.

Services
Power System Study and Analysis
Power System Study and Analysis
Find Out What It's All About

Electrical safety inspections, electrical system design simulations, power quality analysis studies, and power system study and analysis are just a few of the electrical installation services that CareLabs delivers. Our group of electrical specialists employs ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the most useful tool for power system transient analysis, to carry out load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies. It enables us to work directly with customers to build and execute the finest power compensation and solutions. 

Enquire
The Value of Doing a Power System Analysis

Every industrial and commercial structure must have a sturdy, dependable, and well-maintained electrical system. Even a momentary interruption of electricity can have severe implications, leading to protracted downtime, damage to key apparatus, and costly repairs. A continual evaluation of your facility’s electrical system may reveal that it is reliable and secure under both normal and abnormal conditions.

At every stage of the design, installation, and operation of a power system, detailed investigation is necessary. ETAP will be utilized in our studies and evaluations of power systems to provide an overview of the system’s performance during both normal operation and transient fault scenarios. It represents a collection of original engineering analytical studies used to examine how an electric power system responds to events occurring over varying time periods.

Multiple tests indicate the state of the electrical system at your institution. A study report on power system analysis might include the following examples:

Circuit breaker
Coordination of safety procedures
Transit of Load
Dynamic and transient assessment
Arc Flash Examination
Power Efficiency
study of harmonics
Starting the engine
Earthing studies

It allows enterprises to set a baseline for other parts of overall plant management, including the electrical safety program. These investigations indicate the possibility of safe and reliable operations. 

Examination of Load Flow

Using load flow studies, it is feasible to determine the voltages and currents in various network segments and select the appropriate equipment for the task. ETAP’s built-in load flow function may be used to perform load flows, which provide information on a power system’s power loading, power factor, voltages, and currents. 

Short-Circuit Investigation:

A power system must do a short-circuit analysis to comprehend how faulty circumstances emerge. Manually performing short circuit experiments requires considerable work utilizing software like ETAP is significantly more efficient. ETAP is utilized to simulate system protection devices in order to determine the effects of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures in distribution systems. 

Short-Term Study:

Lightning strikes and switching operations cause transient over voltages and currents, which are identified by transient investigations. The results outline the required levels of insulation and surge protection. 

Investigations into Protection Coordination:

This research attempts to validate the system’s security components. This ensures that they are synchronized and appropriate for the apparatus they are guarding.

The following essential functions are often incorporated into our power system analysis procedure:

Data collecting method
Analysis of data and client meetings
accumulating equipment data
Model of a single-line diagram
ETAP power system modelling
Analysis of short circuit and load flow
Investigations into protection cooperation
Arc flash evaluation
A summary of the study report.
Risk assessment and mitigation planning
The administration of a project.

The following components make up the CareLabs Power System Analysis:

Options for reducing energy prices and consumption.
Highlighting issue areas in reports.
Ideas for solutions
Details on how and where energy is utilized.
Verification of safety device ratings
Help in preventing damage and injury.
The effects of harmonics and transients

As part of our power system analysis studies, we analyze the client’s electrical system and then provide recommendations for improving its efficiency and supply security. Zurich, Geneva, Basel, and Bern are served by our power system analysis and study services. Contact us immediately for a quote or to organize a study and analysis of your power system.
